goal: Halstead v2 port + category cleanup: rename information.volume → structural.difficulty.volume, information.difficulty → structural.difficulty.density; port both to view-native with per-grammar operator/operand vocabulary on Language; retire information.* category

summary.title: Halstead v2 port + information.* category retirement
summary.what: Ported both Halstead rules (Volume V and Density D — the latter relabeled from 'difficulty' to better describe operand-reuse density) to v2 view-native. Retired the information.* category and introduced structural.difficulty.* as a sibling to structural.complexity.* — preserving the methodological distinction between control-flow complexity (McCabe/Campbell/Nejmeh) and vocabulary/symbol-density complexity (Halstead). Added per-grammar Language.operator_nodes() and Language.operand_nodes() classmethods with declarations on all 11 grammars (data lifted verbatim from legacy _HalsteadLangConfig). The new rules walk each callable's BODY (excluding function name + parameter list — Halstead operates over function bodies, not signatures). Compat shim translates the four legacy names (information.volume / information.difficulty / halstead.volume / halstead.difficulty) to the new canonical names. Parity verified against legacy halstead_kernel across all 11 supported languages; zero mismatches. 491 tests pass.
summary.why: Two motions bundled: (a) the information.* category was a 2-rule category after the magic_literals relocation, and the user signaled no near-term plans to add more information-theoretic measures; (b) the Halstead V/D rules were ripe for v2 port (same complexity-family template established by cyclomatic/cognitive). The structural.difficulty.* naming was a user-proposed refinement that preserves the McCabe-vs-Halstead methodological distinction better than my initial 'fold into structural.complexity.*' proposal would have. 'Density' as the leaf name for D was chosen over 'difficulty' because it describes what the metric measures (operand-reuse density) rather than the anthropomorphic name from Halstead's 1977 paper.
summary.user_goal: Halstead v2 port + category cleanup: rename information.volume → structural.difficulty.volume, information.difficulty → structural.difficulty.density; port both to view-native with per-grammar operator/operand vocabulary on Language; retire information.* category

decisions.chose: structural.difficulty.* sub-family rather than folding Halstead into structural.complexity.*.
decisions.rationale: User pointed out that McCabe/Campbell/Nejmeh control-flow complexity is methodologically distinct from Halstead vocabulary/density. A shared 'complexity' namespace would erase that. Sub-family inherits structural.* namespacing work and has room for more Halstead-family rules (effort, time) without further renames.

Halstead's published name 'difficulty' anthropomorphizes — suggesting cognitive difficulty for humans, which the metric doesn't actually measure. The formula D = (η₁/2)·(N₂/η₂) quantifies operand-reuse density (how many times each unique operand is reused, weighted by half the operator vocabulary). 'Density' is descriptive and parses cleanly under the 'difficulty' sub-family (the sub-family describes the dimension; the leaf describes the measurement angle).

decisions.chose: Walk function BODY (via _resolve_body), not the whole function_definition.
decisions.rationale: Initial v2 implementation walked the entire function_definition node, which included the function name and parameter identifiers as operands. Legacy walked body only, yielding lower V/D values. Surfaced by parity sweep; fixed by reusing the _resolve_body helper from cyclomatic/cognitive (also honours C++ template_declaration unwrap).

decisions.chose: Inline operator/operand sets as plain string literals in grammar overrides; do NOT add to the Literal/Operator/etc. enums.
decisions.rationale: The legacy data is ~50-100 leaf-token-type strings per grammar, many of which are punctuation operators ('==', '&&', '<-', ':=') that don't map naturally to enum member names. The ast enums are intended for SEMANTIC node types (callables, scopes, control-flow constructs); operator/operand token-level type strings live in a different conceptual layer. Inlining keeps the enum surface focused.

decisions.chose: Delete legacy tests/test_rules/test_halstead.py rather than retrofit for v2 signature.
decisions.rationale: Legacy tests exercise the (root, RuleConfig, SlopConfig) signature against the legacy kernel. v2 needs Structure-based fixtures. Rewriting was cheaper than retrofitting and gives tighter, multi-language tests.

Users invoking `slop check information` from CLI should still resolve to the Halstead pair. The category alias is a CLI/UX concern, distinct from the rule-name canonicalisation handled by LEGACY_RULE_NAMES.

review_notes: Cross-language parity sweep: 11/11 languages match legacy halstead_kernel on canonical 3-decision arithmetic fixture. V values: 76.1 (most C-family + Python/JS/TS/Java/C#), 72.6 (Ruby/Rust due to grammar-specific leaf token differences), 57.0 (Julia due to operator-as-named-child quirk).
Found and fixed a real bug during parity testing: initial v2 walked entire function_definition; legacy walks body only. _resolve_body reuse closed the gap.
Port status: 7 view-native (cyclomatic, cognitive, god_module, imposters, magic_literals, volume, density), 24 shim-wrapped. information.* category is gone.
structural.* now has two complexity sub-families: structural.complexity.{cyclomatic, cognitive, npath} (control-flow) and structural.difficulty.{volume, density} (Halstead).

test_prefix_severity_propagates needed an update — the [rules.structural] prefix now DOES propagate to structural.difficulty.volume (it's under structural.*). The 'doesn't propagate' assertion was switched to lexical.stutter (different top-level namespace).

architectural_claims: structural.complexity.* and structural.difficulty.* are sibling sub-families of structural.* — control-flow complexity (McCabe et al.) vs vocabulary/density complexity (Halstead).

Language.operator_nodes() and Language.operand_nodes() classify tree-sitter LEAF node types for Halstead vocabulary counts. The token-level type-string layer is distinct from the semantic node-type enums in slop.language.ast.
Halstead metrics operate over function bodies (resolved via _resolve_body), not the full function_definition. The function name and parameter list are signature elements, not body content.
behavioral_changes: Halstead D is now named 'density', describing the metric better than 'difficulty'.
Both Halstead rules run view-native; identical V/D values to legacy across 11 supported languages.
[rules.structural] prefix now propagates to both Halstead rules (they live under structural.*).
migration_notes: Configs with [rules.information.volume] / [rules.information.difficulty]: rename to [rules.structural.difficulty.volume] / [rules.structural.difficulty.density]. Legacy names accepted via compat shim but emit deprecation signal.
`slop check information` still resolves to the Halstead pair via LEGACY_CATEGORIES; `slop check halstead` likewise.
